title: PulteGroup, Inc. (PHM) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of PulteGroup, Inc. (PHM) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6% growth rate assumes steady, normalized demand supported by demographic trends and a structural housing deficit, offset by cyclical fluctuations in the housing market.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.5% discount rate reflects the inherent cyclicality and capital intensity of the homebuilding industry, as well as its high sensitivity to macroeconomic factors like interest rates.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate is used, representing long-term population growth and historical housing formation rates, acknowledging the cyclical nature of the industry.

thesisParagraphs: PulteGroup is strategically positioned to capitalize on the chronic undersupply of housing in the United States. Years of underbuilding following the Global Financial Crisis have created a structural deficit that will take years to resolve. PHM, with its national scale and diverse portfolio targeting different buyer segments, is a prime beneficiary.

The company's transition to a more asset-light land model represents a fundamental improvement in its business quality. By controlling land through options rather than owning it outright, PHM has reduced its balance sheet risk and significantly improved its return on invested capital, making it a more resilient enterprise across housing cycles.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How do higher mortgage rates affect this valuation?
faq.answer: Higher rates pressure affordability. However, the model assumes large builders like PHM use their scale to offer financing incentives (rate buydowns), sustaining volume even if it slightly impacts gross margins.

faq.question: Why is the discount rate relatively high?
faq.answer: Homebuilding is a deeply cyclical industry sensitive to interest rates, employment, and consumer confidence. The 9.5% discount rate compensates for this inherent economic sensitivity.

faq.question: What is the impact of PHM's 'asset-light' strategy?
faq.answer: By optioning land instead of buying it years in advance, PHM ties up less capital. This leads to higher free cash flow generation and provides flexibility to walk away from land if market conditions deteriorate.
